title: 🧩subworkflow - aws get process data of process running on ec2 instance
description:
Subworkflow to get data of a process running on an aws ec2 instance without needing to know DQL or Javascript

An easy way to get additional data of a process running on an aws ec2 instance
such as process name, ec2 region, ec2 instance id, ec2 volume id, tags etc.
which is needed to for example to remediate a problem created by a process
running on an ec2 instance.

Parameters:

process_group_instance_id:  (Required) the dynatrace process group instance id
guide:
## 🧬 **Subworkflow to Get Data of a Process Running on an AWS EC2 Instance (No DQL or JavaScript Required)**

🔍 This subworkflow provides an **easy, DQL‑free and JavaScript‑free way** to retrieve detailed information about a **process running on an AWS EC2 instance** from Dynatrace.

☁️ The integration leverages the **Dynatrace AWS Gen3 connector**, ensuring secure, scalable, and modern access to AWS and process-level metadata.

It is commonly used to collect information such as:
- 🧾 Process name  
- 🌍 AWS region  
- 🆔 EC2 instance ID  
- 💽 Associated volume IDs  
- 🏷️ Tags and related metadata  

➡️ This data is typically required for **process-driven remediation**, root cause analysis, or automation workflows that respond to problems caused by processes running on EC2 instances.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **process_group_instance_id** (Required): 🧬 The Dynatrace **Process Group Instance ID** representing the running process.

---

### 🧠 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Uses the **Dynatrace AWS Gen3 connector**
- ✅ Eliminates the need to write **DQL or JavaScript**
- ✅ Enables process-aware automation and remediation
- ✅ Designed as a **helper / data-enrichment subworkflow**
- ✅ Commonly paired with process remediation, validation, or escalation workflows

---

input.query:
fetch dt.entity.process_group_instance, from:now()-120h
| filter matchesphrase(id,"{{input()["process_group_instance_id"] }}")
| fieldsadd process_group_id=instance_of[dt.entity.process_group],host.id=belongs_to[dt.entity.host],  bitness, ebpfHasPublicTraffic, processType,metadata, entity.type, tags, lifetime, softwareTechnologies //process_called_by=called_by, calls
| lookup[fetch dt.entity.host |fieldsadd  additionalSystemInfo,belongs_to,ec2_id=runs_on[dt.entity.ec2_instance], entity.name, hostname=entity.detected_name],sourceField:host.id, lookupField:id, prefix:"hostdata_"
| lookup  [fetch dt.entity.ebs_volume
| fieldsadd arn,instanceid=belongs_to[dt.entity.ec2_instance][0],deviceName,ebsType,entity.name,awsNameTag,tags,belongs_to,boshName,creationTimestamp, lifetime, ebsSnapshotId, entity.type ] , sourceField:hostdata_ec2_id, lookupField:instanceid, prefix:"ebs_"
| fieldsadd volumeid=ebs_entity.name
| lookup  [fetch dt.entity.ec2_instance
| fieldsadd awsInstanceId,awsInstanceType,awsNameTag, awsSecurityGroup, awsVpcName, arn, tags, id, regionName, accessible_by,amiId,balanced_by,beanstalkEnvironmentName,belongs_to,boshName,contains,publicHostName,publicIp,localIp,virtualizedDiskType,entity.name,lifetime, entity.type  ] , sourceField:hostdata_ec2_id, lookupField:id, prefix:"ec2_"
| lookup  [ timeseries max(dt.host.disk.used), by: { dt.entity.host }
| fieldsAdd disksizex = arrayLast(`max(dt.host.disk.used)`)/1000000000
| fields disksize=round(disksizex), dt.entity.host
] , sourceField:id, lookupField:dt.entity.host, prefix:"disk_"
